on

By

Cameroon head coach Jean-Baptiste Bisseck is confident in the ability of his side to beat the Super Falcons of Nigeria on the road to the Olympics.

The Indomitable Lionesses face a daunting task in their bid to qualify for the 2024 Olympic Games after they were held to a barren draw in the first leg last Friday.

Ahead of the second leg slated for the Moshood Abiola Stadium, Abuja, on Monday, Bisseck has issued a warning to the nine-time African champions.

“We will play on the Abuja field as if we are playing at home. Cameroon will not entertain any fear. The Lionesses are prepared to win and make progress,” the gaffer was quoted by thenff.com.

African champions South Africa or Tanzania await the winner of the fixture in the final round.

South Africa defeated Tanzania 3-0 in the first leg.
